Nice to meet you, Daniel. Let me introduce myself, I'm Maria Clara, from Brazil. I lead a team at a federal university.
A pleasure. So, is this your first time in Seoul? How are you finding the conversation at these dinners?
It's my first time, and I love it. The host is wonderful, and every guest is so friendly. It makes a great first impression.

I'm responsible for institutional partnerships. We work closely with international delegations. And what about you?
I lead the international office. That's a great topic for us, and it is very polite of you to ask. Maybe we can continue this conversation later.

How are you finding the conference?

Good evening, everyone, and welcome to our conference dinner. My name is Sophia, and I am your host tonight. It is a real pleasure to see so many guests from around the world at the same table. Tonight is not about presentations. It is about conversation. So please, introduce yourself to the person next to you. Ask an easy question. Talk about your day, about the city, or about your background. A good dinner starts with good small talk. So relax, enjoy the evening, and make a wonderful first impression.

Hi Maria Clara, it is a quick message before the dinner tonight. I know these events can feel a little scary, so here is one simple idea. Small talk is not a test. It has rules, and you can learn them. Open with an easy question, like: is this your first time here? Then listen, react, and ask one question back. If you want to change the topic, just say: by the way. And please remember, it is polite to ask about the other person too. You are going to be great. See you there.
